title: IncidentReport
description: Canonical shape consumed by Container 1 (Problem Report). Every trigger source — vision API, ERP event, manual portal, supplier deviation report — normalizes its input to this shape before entering the BPMN. The Analyst agent, IRB Action Center HITL, and downstream containers all read this object.

incidentId.description: Stable identifier assigned by the trigger adapter. Format: `IR-{YYYYMMDD}-{seq}` recommended.

type.description: Trigger source category. Determines which adapter normalized the input.

detectorConfidence.description: When applicable (vision, automated detectors), the source's confidence score. Used by the Risk Agent's Fast Track gateway. Omit or set to 1.0 for manual/deterministic sources.

occurredAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp when the underlying event occurred (not when the trigger fired the BPMN).

severity.description: Initial severity tag from the trigger source. The Risk Agent may revise this in Container 3.

subject.description: Short human-readable headline (e.g., `NG_WIRING: misrouted cable, panel A-12`).

description.description: Free-form details supplied by the source. May include error codes, observed measurements, comparison to spec, attached evidence URIs.

affectedItems.description: BOM-level items implicated by the incident. Drives supplier impact analysis in Container 4.

itemId.description: PLM part number, BOM line, or supplier catalog number.

supplierId.description: Optional supplier identifier; populated when an item is sourced externally.

quantity.description: Affected quantity or count of units in the affected lot.

complianceClass.description: Regulated-industry compliance class of the affected items. Drives the Risk Agent (high compliance class biases toward Full Track / CRB HITL).

evidence.description: Supporting evidence URIs (defect images, ERP screenshots, supplier deviation PDFs).

uri.description: Cloud storage or content-addressable URI to the evidence artifact.

reportedBy.description: Human or system that filed the incident (the `Requestor` role in the framework).

principalId.description: User ID, service account name, or detector instance ID.

tags.description: Free-form tags used by the Analyst agent for clustering related incidents.

vendor.description: Trigger-source-specific raw payload, preserved verbatim for audit. Adapters MUST NOT mutate; downstream agents may consult.
